title: Flexible spinal cord stimulators for pain and trauma management through neuromodulation
abstract: A spinal cord stimulator includes: (1) a flexible substrate; (2) a power source embedded in the flexible substrate; (3) a controller embedded in the flexible substrate and connected to the power source; and (4) an array of electrodes, including an array of stimulation electrodes, disposed over the flexible substrate and connected to the controller, wherein the controller is configured to direct the array of stimulation electrodes to deliver a stimulation pattern to a spinal cord of a patient.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A spinal cord stimulator comprising: a flexible substrate; a power source embedded in the flexible substrate; a controller embedded in the flexible substrate and connected to the power source; and an array of electrodes, including an array of stimulation electrodes, disposed over the flexible substrate and connected to the controller, wherein the controller is configured to direct the array of stimulation electrodes to deliver a stimulation patern to a spinal cord of a patient. 2. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 1, wherein the flexible substrate includes a flexible material. 3. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 2, wherein the flexible material is an elastomer or a gel. 4. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 2, wherein the flexible material has a Youngâs modulus of up to 3 MPa. 5. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 2, wherein the flexible material has a percentage elongation-at-break of at least 5%. 6. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 1, wherein the array of electrodes further includes an array of sensing electrodes. 7. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 6, wherein the controller is configured to adjust the stimulation patern responsive to signals received from the array of sensing electrodes. 8. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 7, wherein the controller is configured to perform machine learning, responsive to the signals received from the array of sensing electrodes, to 

 determine a set of stimulation parameters, and the controller is configured to adjust the stimulation pattern according to the set of stimulation parameters. 9. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 7, wherein the controller includes a neuromorphic computing component. 10. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 9, wherein the neuromorphic computing component includes a set of charge-trapping transistors. 11. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 1 , wherein the power source is a battery. 12. A spinal cord stimulator comprising: a flexible substrate; a power source embedded in the flexible substrate; a controller embedded in the flexible substrate and connected to the power source; an array of stimulation electrodes disposed over the flexible substrate and connected to the controller; and an array of sensing electrodes disposed over the flexible substrate and connected to the controller, wherein the controller is configured to direct the array of stimulation electrodes to deliver a stimulation pattern, and the controller is configured to adjust the stimulation pattern responsive to signals received from the array of sensing electrodes. 13. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 12, wherein the flexible substrate includes an elastomer or a gel. 14. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 12, wherein the power source is a battery. 15. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 12, further comprising a flexible layer disposed over the flexible substrate and defining openings, and the array of stimulation electrodes and the array of sensing electrodes are disposed in the openings of the flexible layer. 16. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 12, wherein the power source includes bonding pads, and the bonding pads of the power source are exposed from the flexible substrate. 17. The spinal cord stimulator of claim 12, wherein the controller includes bonding pads, and the bonding pads of the controller are exposed from the flexible substrate.
